questions: Apple recently introduced an integrated, on-device AI feature suite built into its latest operating systems for Mac and other compatible devices. For a user who wants to enable and fully use this AI system across their Mac and related Apple devices, what are the hardware and software requirements (including minimum chip models and OS versions), storage and language prerequisites, and initial setup steps? Additionally, summarize the major categories of features this AI system powers on Mac, iPhone, Apple Watch, and Apple Vision Pro, and explain any notable regional or device-purchase restrictions that may affect availability.

A new Mac user in Singapore wants to (1) purchase an Apple device through Apple’s official Singapore online store and understand the applicable return, refund, and price-protection conditions, and (2) enable the built-in AI features integrated into macOS and related Apple devices. Based only on publicly available Apple policies and feature documentation, explain the standard return window and key refund limitations for consumer purchases in Singapore (including any conditions about bank accounts, international refunds, product exclusions, volume purchases, and price adjustments). Then, detail the technical and regional requirements for activating Apple’s integrated AI features on a compatible Mac and related devices, including minimum hardware models, operating system versions, storage requirements, language settings, regional availability constraints (such as China mainland and EU limitations), and examples of major AI-powered features available on Mac. Answer length: 230-330 words.

rounds.question: Apple recently introduced an integrated, on-device AI feature suite built into its latest operating systems for Mac and other compatible devices. For a user who wants to enable and fully use this AI system across their Mac and related Apple devices, what are the hardware and software requirements (including minimum chip models and OS versions), storage and language prerequisites, and initial setup steps? Additionally, summarize the major categories of features this AI system powers on Mac, iPhone, Apple Watch, and Apple Vision Pro, and explain any notable regional or device-purchase restrictions that may affect availability.
checklist_width: Requirement to update to the latest supported OS version (e.g., macOS Sequoia 15.1 or later; iOS/iPadOS 18.1 or later; visionOS 2.4 or later; watchOS 11 or later, with newer 26.x references where applicable)
Mac hardware requirement: M1 chip or later
iPhone requirement: iPhone 15 Pro models, iPhone 16 models or later
iPad requirement: M1 or later, or iPad mini with A17 Pro
Apple Watch requirement: Series 6 or later, Ultra models, SE 2 or later when paired with a compatible iPhone
Minimum 7 GB storage requirement (except Apple Watch)
Device language and Siri language must match a supported language
Initial setup steps: update OS, enable feature in Settings, allow on-device models to download (Wi‑Fi and power recommended)
Major Mac features: Writing Tools, Genmoji, Image Playground, Siri enhancements, ChatGPT integration, Smart Reply, summaries, Priority messages, Reduce Interruptions Focus
iPhone-specific capabilities: Visual intelligence (identify places, interact with text, plant/animal ID, calendar from poster), Live Translation (Messages/Phone/FaceTime/AirPods)
watchOS features: Workout Buddy, Live Translation in Messages, notification summaries (when paired with supported iPhone)
visionOS features: Genmoji, Image Playground, Writing Tools, Smart Reply, summaries
Regional restrictions: Not currently functional for supported devices purchased in mainland China; additional limitations when account region and physical location are mainland China
EU limitation: Live Translation with AirPods unavailable for EU residents with EU device and account region
Language availability varies by feature and platform
checklist_depth: Apple Intelligence + Identified as Apple’s on-device AI system integrated into macOS, iOS, iPadOS, visionOS, and watchOS
Mac with M1 or later + Meets the silicon requirement for running on-device AI models
iPhone 15 Pro / iPhone 16 or later + Required for enabling the AI system on iPhone and pairing-dependent features on Apple Watch
Apple Watch Series 6 or later (including Ultra, SE 2+) + Must be paired with an AI-enabled iPhone to access supported features
Apple Vision Pro + Gains AI-powered features beginning with supported visionOS versions
rounds.rationale: The question uses logical filtering by describing an integrated, on-device AI system introduced in recent Apple operating systems without naming it, requiring the agent to infer the correct feature suite (Deep). It then demands aggregation of hardware requirements, OS versions, setup steps, storage and language constraints, cross-device feature comparisons, and regional limitations across multiple platforms (Wide), ensuring the answer cannot be derived from a single isolated fact.
source_nodes: How to get Apple Intelligence - Apple Support
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: To get started with Apple Intelligence features on your compatible iPhone, iPad, Mac, or Apple Vision Pro, update your device to the latest software
rounds.answer_a:
To enable Apple Intelligence, users must meet specific hardware and software prerequisites. 

**Hardware & Software:** The system natively requires an iPhone 15 Pro, 15 Pro Max, or any iPhone 16 model (using A17 Pro or A18 chips), or an iPad or Mac powered by an M1 chip or later [1]. Devices must run iOS 18.1, iPadOS 18.1, or macOS Sequoia 15.1 or newer [2].

**Storage & Language:** Devices require a minimum of 4GB of free storage initially [3]. Presently, both the device language and Siri language must be set to U.S. English [2].

**Setup Steps:** Users navigate to Settings > Apple Intelligence & Siri and tap "Join the Apple Intelligence Waitlist." Once approved automatically within a few minutes, they select "Turn On Apple Intelligence" to download the necessary on-device AI models [3].

**Feature Categories:**
*   **Mac & iPhone:** The suite powers deep OS integration, including Writing Tools (revising, proofreading, and summarizing text), an enhanced Siri with onscreen awareness and richer conversational context, and smart Mail and notification summaries [1][4].
*   **Apple Watch & Apple Vision Pro:** Apple Intelligence does not currently run natively on these devices. However, the Apple Watch benefits indirectly by receiving AI-generated smart notification summaries forwarded from a paired, compatible iPhone [4]. Apple Vision Pro lacks native support today, but future visionOS updates are slated to embed these features natively alongside M2 chip capabilities [5].

**Restrictions:** Apple Intelligence is completely unavailable for devices purchased in mainland China [2]. For the European Union, the feature is officially accessible on Mac, but iPhone and iPad users cannot use it yet unless logged into a U.S. Apple ID. Official EU support for iOS and iPadOS is scheduled for April 2025 [2][3].

## References
[1] Apple Newsroom. (2024). "Apple Intelligence is available today on iPhone, iPad, and Mac." Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/newsroom
[2] Apple Support. (2024). "How to get Apple Intelligence." Retrieved from https://support.apple.com/en-us/121115
[3] MacRumors. (2024). "Apple Intelligence: The Ultimate Guide." Retrieved from https://www.macrumors.com/guide/apple-intelligence/
[4] The Verge. (2024). "Apple Intelligence is now available for the iPhone, iPad, and Mac." Retrieved from https://www.theverge.com
[5] 9to5Mac. (2024). "Apple Intelligence is coming to Vision Pro, but not this year." Retrieved from https://9to5mac.com
rounds.answer_b:
Apple’s new integrated on‑device AI suite (“Apple Intelligence” and the new Siri) requires:

**Hardware & OS requirements**  
- **Mac:** Apple silicon (M1 or later) running **macOS Sequoia** (macOS 15) or later. [1][2]  
- **iPhone:** **iPhone 15 Pro or 15 Pro Max** with **iOS 18** or later. [1]  
- **iPad:** iPad with **M1 or later** chip and **iPadOS 18** or later. [1][2]  
- **Apple Watch / Vision Pro:** Benefit mainly via continuity features when paired with a supported iPhone/Mac running the above OS versions; watchOS/visionOS themselves are not primary Apple‑Intelligence hosts as of initial release. [1][3]

**Storage, language, region**  
- Some AI features require free local storage for model downloads (Apple has not published an exact minimum, but expect several GB).  
- Initial release supports **U.S. English** only, with more languages/regions “over time.” [1][2]  
- Features like ChatGPT integration may require an OpenAI connection and may be limited in some countries. [1]

**Initial setup steps**  
1. Update eligible devices to iOS 18 / iPadOS 18 / macOS Sequoia.  
2. During setup or in **Settings > Privacy & Security > Apple Intelligence** (name/location may change), opt in to Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute. [1][2]  
3. Ensure Siri language is set to U.S. English and enable “Use Apple Intelligence” where prompted.  
4. Sign in with the same Apple ID across devices and enable iCloud sync.

**Major feature categories**  
- **Systemwide writing tools** (rewrite, proofread, summarize in apps). [1][2]  
- **Image features** (Image Playground, Genmoji, image editing). [1]  
- **Notification and content prioritization** (Priority Messages, summaries). [2]  
- **Smarter Siri** with richer context, app actions, on‑device understanding. [1]  
- **Cross‑device continuity**: using these AI enhancements from Mac to iPhone, with supported experiences flowing to Apple Watch and Vision Pro via paired devices. [1][3]

Notable restrictions: only on Apple silicon / latest high‑end iPhones; U.S. English and select regions at launch; some online features may depend on region‑specific cloud availability. [1][2]

## References  
[1] Apple – “Apple Intelligence is coming to iPhone, iPad, and Mac” (WWDC24 announcement page)  
[2] Apple – macOS Sequoia / iOS 18 / iPadOS 18 preview pages (feature and compatibility sections)  
[3] Apple – Apple Vision Pro and watchOS feature pages (continuity and ecosystem notes)
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identify Apple Intelligence as Apple’s on-device AI system integrated across macOS, iOS, etc., so both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both miss several checklist items (e.g., 7 GB storage minimum, detailed watchOS/visionOS version requirements, iPad mini A17 Pro, full feature matrix like Visual Intelligence, Live Translation, Workout Buddy, China + EU nuances). However, Agent B omits more required specifics: no mention of iPhone 16 models, no concrete storage figure, no China device-purchase restriction, no EU AirPods Live Translation limitation, and minimal coverage of Vision Pro/watchOS features. Agent A, while factually flawed in places (incorrect 4GB storage, outdated waitlist flow, incorrect claim that Vision Pro lacks native support), still provides more detailed setup steps, regional restrictions (China + EU), and a broader feature breakdown.

Presentation & UX: Agent A is more structured, more scannable, and richer in feature explanation. Agent B is comparatively vague (“several GB,” “may change,” “benefit mainly via continuity”) and less actionable.

Conclusion: Both have factual gaps (so not MUCH_BETTER), but Agent A provides a more comprehensive and useful user experience. Agent B loses primarily due to missing major checklist details (WIDE failure).

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question: A new Mac user in Singapore wants to (1) purchase an Apple device through Apple’s official Singapore online store and understand the applicable return, refund, and price-protection conditions, and (2) enable the built-in AI features integrated into macOS and related Apple devices. Based only on publicly available Apple policies and feature documentation, explain the standard return window and key refund limitations for consumer purchases in Singapore (including any conditions about bank accounts, international refunds, product exclusions, volume purchases, and price adjustments). Then, detail the technical and regional requirements for activating Apple’s integrated AI features on a compatible Mac and related devices, including minimum hardware models, operating system versions, storage requirements, language settings, regional availability constraints (such as China mainland and EU limitations), and examples of major AI-powered features available on Mac. Answer length: 230-330 words.
checklist_width: 14-day standard return window for non-volume consumer purchases in Singapore
Requirement that refunds be remitted only to a Singapore bank account matching the payor and not outside Singapore
Products that are not eligible for return (e.g., electronic software downloads, certain subscriptions, developer products)
7-day return window and 25% restocking fee for volume purchases (more than 4 per category)
14-day price protection policy and requirement to contact support within 14 days of price change (with exclusions such as limited-time promotions)
Mac with M1 or later required for Apple Intelligence
macOS Sequoia 15.1 or later (and macOS 26/Tahoe 26 for expanded features)
Approximately 7 GB of on-device storage required (not for Apple Watch)
Device language and Siri language must match a supported language
Regional limitations (not working on supported devices purchased in China mainland; limitations when Apple Account region is China mainland; EU restriction for Live Translation with AirPods)
Examples of AI features on Mac (e.g., Writing Tools, Genmoji, Image Playground, Smart Reply, summaries in Mail/Messages, Siri enhancements, ChatGPT integration, natural language search in Photos)
checklist_depth: Apple Online Store Singapore Consumer Policy + identification via Singapore-specific refund, shipping, export, and sales restrictions
Apple Intelligence on Mac + logic that it requires Mac with M1 or later and specified macOS versions, with regional and language constraints
rounds.rationale: The question uses the Mac onboarding context (Deep) to logically connect two separate but related domains: Singapore consumer purchase policies and activation of Apple Intelligence on Mac. It avoids naming any specific source document while requiring identification of the Singapore store policy and the Apple Intelligence compatibility page. The task is Wide because the respondent must aggregate multiple categories of details: return rules, refund mechanics, price protection, hardware/software requirements, storage, language alignment, regional restrictions, and feature examples across Mac and related Apple devices.
source_nodes: How to get Apple Intelligence - Apple Support
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Singapore Consumer Store Thanks for shopping at Apple. We appreciate the fact that you like to buy the cool stuff we build. We also want to make sure
rounds.answer_a:
Apple Singapore Online Store purchases can be returned within 14 calendar days of receipt for a full refund [1]. Excluded products include electronic software downloads, Apple Print Products, and unsealed boxed software [1]. Notably, volume purchases involving more than four identical items face a strictly shortened 7-day return window [1]. For price protection, if Apple reduces a product's official retail price within 14 days of a customer receiving it, buyers can claim a refund for the price difference by contacting support within 14 days of the announcement [1]. Apple issues wire transfer refunds exclusively to Singapore-based bank accounts; refunds to international bank accounts are prohibited [1].

To successfully activate Apple Intelligence on a compatible device, users require a Mac or iPad featuring an M1 chip or newer, an iPhone 16 model, or an iPhone 15 Pro or Pro Max [2]. Hardware must be updated to macOS Sequoia 15.1, iOS 18.1, or iPadOS 18.1 or later [2]. Additionally, device setups require at least 4GB of initial free storage, and both the device language and Siri must initially be configured to U.S. English [2].

Regionally, Apple Intelligence remains fundamentally disabled for Apple devices purchased in mainland China [2]. Within the European Union, the AI suite is officially available on Macs; however, it remains restricted on iPhones and iPads unless the user is securely signed into a U.S.-based Apple ID. Full EU support for mobile devices is scheduled to roll out in April 2025 [2].

Once enabled, compatible Macs receive powerful built-in AI capabilities. Major features include system-wide Writing Tools that can rewrite, elegantly proofread, or perfectly summarize text across nearly any application [2]. The OS update also introduces intelligent priority sorting and smart replies in the Mail app, alongside a deeply enhanced Siri featuring advanced conversational context and natural language understanding [2].

## References
[1] Apple Singapore. (2024). "Returns & Refunds." Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[2] Apple Support. (2024). "How to get Apple Intelligence." Retrieved from https://support.apple.com/en-us/121115
rounds.answer_b:
For Apple’s Singapore online store, most hardware and accessories purchased by consumers can be returned within **14 calendar days** from the date you receive the item. [1] Items must be in good condition; opened software, electronic software downloads, personalized products, and gift cards are generally **non‑returnable**. [1] Refunds are issued to the **original method of payment**; Apple will not refund to a different bank account or card. For credit‑card or bank refunds, timing depends on the issuing bank, and cross‑border or foreign‑currency transactions are subject to your bank’s exchange rates and fees; Apple refunds only the SGD amount it originally charged. [1] Large “volume” or business purchases may be governed by separate terms and are not covered by consumer‑oriented price protections. Apple Singapore does **not** advertise a general post‑purchase **price‑adjustment** policy; discounted prices usually apply only at the time of order. [1]

To use Apple’s integrated AI (“Apple Intelligence”) on Mac, you need a Mac with **Apple silicon (M1 or later)** running **macOS Sequoia (macOS 15)** or later. [2] iPhone features require **iPhone 15 Pro / 15 Pro Max** with **iOS 18**; iPad features require an **M1‑or‑later iPad** with **iPadOS 18**. [2] Apple notes that models may download AI components, so you should keep **several gigabytes** of free storage. Apple Intelligence initially supports **U.S. English** only, and Apple states it will be available “in beta on iPhone, iPad, and Mac in the U.S.” at first, with additional regions later. [2][3] Some capabilities are **unavailable in mainland China** and may face **regulatory delays or adjustments in the EU/EEA**, particularly for advanced Siri and app‑integration features. [3]

On a supported Mac, update to macOS Sequoia, set system and Siri language to **English (US)**, and enable Apple Intelligence/Private Cloud Compute in System Settings. Key Mac features include system‑wide writing tools (rewrite, proofread, summarize), AI‑powered notification and email prioritization, Image Playground and Genmoji, and a more contextual Siri that can understand on‑screen content and perform app actions. [2][3]

## References  
[1] Apple Singapore – Sales & Refunds Policy / Online Store Terms  
[2] Apple – “Apple Intelligence is coming to iPhone, iPad, and Mac” (WWDC24)  
[3] Apple – iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS Sequoia preview and regional availability notes
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identified the relevant entities—Apple Singapore Online Store return/refund policies and Apple Intelligence requirements for Mac—so both pass the core entity check.

Second, Width/Completeness: Agent A correctly states the 14-day return window, 7-day limit for volume purchases, Singapore-only refund remittance, and a 14-day price-protection policy. However, A misses the 25% restocking fee for volume purchases and understates storage (4GB instead of ~7GB), and omits some regional nuances. Agent B correctly covers the 14-day window and M1/macOS requirements, but fails a major checklist item by stating Apple Singapore does not advertise a general price-adjustment policy—contradicting the required 14-day price protection. B also omits the 7-day/25% volume purchase rule and the Singapore bank-account restriction. These are significant WIDE aggregation failures.

Finally, User Experience & Presentation: Agent B has slightly better formatting and structure, but its incorrect claim about price protection is a substantive policy error. Agent A, despite minor inaccuracies, aligns more closely with the ground-truth checklist and covers more required sub-points correctly.

Therefore, Agent A provides the better overall answer, but not at the MUCH_BETTER level due to its factual and completeness gaps.
